items.title: Canada-EU: Closer trade ties to counter Trump?
items.description: Given they share the world's longest land border, Canada and the US have historically enjoyed fruitful trade relations. But with Donald Trump escalating his tariff war, Canada is looking to the EU and further afield.

items.title: U.S. Justice Department withdraws NY Times subpoenas over Trump plane report
items.description: The newspaper had challenged the government requests for phone records of reporters and grand jury testimony as an attempt to "intimidate" the press and filed a motion to have them quashed

items.title: Selección given warm welcome home as thousands line streets in thanks
items.description: Thousands of fans brave cold and rain to give a rapturous welcome to Argentina's World Cup squad. Leer más

items.title: Socceroos star allegedly tests positive for cocaine after speeding
items.description: Police say they stopped the 22-year-old in a BMW Coupe about 1am today and allege he was travelling 109 kilometres per hour on the Anzac Bridge.

items.title: Hiding behind the World Cup, Milei siblings restructure their government
items.description: The World Cup provided the Milei administration a much-needed breather after three months under siege. Javier and Karina Milei are learning by doing, but by doing so they are slowly but surely resembling what they initially promised to battle. Leer más

items.title: US citizens are ramping up pressure on data centers
items.description: Rapid data center construction across the US, fueled by AI, comes with serious threats to the environment, public health and energy supply. Local opposition has blocked or delayed dozens of projects.

items.title: Nigeria, Germany agree to deepen business, trade ties
items.description: Germany's foreign minister wrapped up a two-day visit to Nigeria Wednesday as Berlin moves to deepen business opportunities across Africa amid a more isolationist United States and geo-political pressure from Russia and China

items.title: Senegal set to make Olympic history with Dakar 2026 Youth Games
items.description: The Youth Olympic Games open in Dakar on 31 October. Africa has produced Olympic legends for decades, but has never hosted the Games themselves, until now.

items.title: U.S. signs commercial nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia
items.description: The agreement between the U.S. and Saudi Arabia gives American companies priority access to nuclear reactors and fuel to Saudi Arabia. It's expected to last decades and be worth billions of dollars.

items.title: Israelis fear Saudi nuclear deal could ignite West Asia arms race
items.description: The agreement, ⁠which still needs Congressional approval, that was announced on July 22, is meant to allow the Saudis to develop a civilian nuclear programme.

items.title: How Russia is luring foreign nationals to fight in Ukraine
items.description: More and more, Russia is recruiting men from the world’s poorest countries to send them to the front in the Ukraine war. Why are people joining up?

items.title: Google and Tesla shares plunge as AI spending rattles markets
items.description: As major tech companies race to capitalize on a new wave of AI technology, investors wonder when financial benefits will appear.

items.title: What's next for the Albiceleste after World Cup final defeat?
items.description: Argentina fell agonisingly short of winning a second consecutive World Cup title. As the team reaches the end of an era, speculation begins over what comes next. Leer más
